name: Compound Interest Calculator
description: Industrial-grade compound interest calculator with lean and six sigma principles for financial forecasting and investment analysis.

inputs.label: Principal Amount
inputs.businessContext: Initial investment or loan amount in base currency.

businessContext_i18n.en: Initial investment or loan amount in base currency.

inputs.label: Annual Interest Rate
inputs.businessContext: Nominal annual interest rate as a percentage.

businessContext_i18n.en: Nominal annual interest rate as a percentage.

inputs.label: Compounding Frequency
inputs.businessContext: Number of compounding periods per year (e.g., 1=annual, 12=monthly, 365=daily).

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of compounding periods per year (e.g., 1=annual, 12=monthly, 365=daily).

inputs.label: Time Period
inputs.businessContext: Total duration of investment or loan in years.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total duration of investment or loan in years.

inputs.label: Additional Contribution per Period
inputs.businessContext: Fixed amount added at the end of each compounding period.

businessContext_i18n.en: Fixed amount added at the end of each compounding period.

inputs.label: Inflation Rate
inputs.businessContext: Expected annual inflation rate for real value adjustment.

businessContext_i18n.en: Expected annual inflation rate for real value adjustment.

inputs.label: Tax Rate on Interest
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of interest earnings taxed annually.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of interest earnings taxed annually.

rules.message: Principal must be greater than zero.
Annual interest rate cannot be negative.
Time period must be greater than zero.
Compounding frequency must be between 1 and 365.

thresholds.message: Interest rate exceeds typical market rates; verify input.
Long-term projection beyond 30 years may have reduced accuracy.
Inflation rate is unusually high; consider economic context.

formulas.name: Effective Annual Rate (EAR)
formulas.description: Converts nominal rate to effective annual rate considering compounding frequency.

formulas.name: Future Value of Additional Contributions
formulas.description: Future value of a series of equal payments made at the end of each compounding period.

formulas.name: Gross Future Value
formulas.description: Total future value before tax and inflation adjustments.

formulas.name: Tax Adjustment Factor
formulas.description: Factor to reduce future value by tax on interest earned.

formulas.name: Net Future Value (After Tax)
formulas.description: Future value after deducting taxes on interest.

formulas.name: Real Future Value (Inflation Adjusted)
formulas.description: Future value in today's purchasing power.

primary.label: Real Future Value (Inflation & Tax Adjusted)
primary.description: Primary result: future value adjusted for inflation and taxes.

inflationLoss.label: Loss Due to Inflation
inflationLoss.description: Reduction in purchasing power due to inflation.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Inflation Erosion
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Inflation reduces real purchasing power over time.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Tax Drag
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Taxes on interest reduce net returns.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Opportunity Cost of Low Compounding Frequency
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Less frequent compounding reduces potential growth.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Compounding Frequency
suggestedActions.description: Switching from annual to monthly compounding can increase returns.

suggestedActions.label: Tax-Efficient Investing
suggestedActions.description: Consider tax-advantaged accounts to minimize tax drag.

suggestedActions.label: Inflation-Protected Investments
suggestedActions.description: Invest in assets that historically outpace inflation (e.g., equities, TIPS).

suggestedActions.label: Increase Additional Contributions
suggestedActions.description: Regular contributions significantly boost future value.
